current_implemented_build: V52 Model Registry Foundation and Human-First Executive Control View
release_chain_status: V52 implemented the human-first executive control view, modular app orchestration, registry bundle loading, model-aware advisory scoring, and updated README and bots metadata. No new top-level tabs were added.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
version_label_rule: Build and version labels belong in Settings/admin metadata and repo documentation only. They must not appear as executive-facing dashboard content.
core_thesis: AI is controlled before it acts, or it is not controlled at all.
core_question: Should this action be operationally admissible right now?
v51_positioning_line: When everything can be faked, CyberShield helps prove what can be trusted.
v52_changes: Executive First View added inside Briefing
model trace exposed through progressive disclosure
Proof Pack includes model context and prototype boundary

implemented_release_train.v52: Model Registry Foundation and Human-First Executive Control View

prototype_boundary: The current public build is a static advisory prototype. It models decisions and proof records but does not perform production enforcement or live enterprise integrations.
known_limitations: CSS remains inline in index.html because connector writes for new CSS files were intermittently blocked during V52.
The V52 scoring model is demo-directional and not statistically validated.
The current registry foundation uses scaffold files and is not a complete framework control mapping library.
Hands-on Firefox, Brave, and Android QA still needs to be performed after GitHub Pages deploys.
